description: AGT-11: 'do I have a leads table? what's in it?' — the assistant must actually surface the rows it pulled (the real values), not just confirm the table exists or state a count. Pins the show-the-data-don't-just-claim-it behavior. Judgment-heavy -> capability.
userTurns: do I have a leads table? what's in it?

judge.rubric: The user asked whether a Leads table exists AND what's in it. PASS when the reply shows the ACTUAL data it retrieved — i.e. it presents the specific rows/values (company names like Acme Corp / Globex / Initech, or their emails/stages), so the user sees what's in the table. FAIL when it only confirms the table exists, only gives a count ('you have a Leads table with 3 rows'), describes the columns/schema without any row values, or claims it has the data without showing any of it. Summarizing or formatting the rows nicely is a PASS; showing zero concrete row values is a FAIL.
